canonical_definition: Based on the available data, LongFact is characterized as a dataset designed to evaluate the factuality of language models, specifically within long-form generation tasks. It is defined as containing 250 prompts and is described as one of several "fact-seeking long-form generation datasets." The primary application shown in the provided sources is its use as a measurement instrument for Hallucination. In this capacity, it is explicitly identified as a "Factuality hallucination benchmark," operationalizing the assessment of factual accuracy in extended model outputs.
papers: Disambiguating Reference in Visually Grounded Dialogues through Joint Modeling of Textual and Multimodal Semantic Structures
LIFBench: Evaluating the Instruction Following Performance and Stability of Large Language Models in Long-Context Scenarios
MemeQA: Holistic Evaluation for Meme Understanding
OMGM: Orchestrate Multiple Granularities and Modalities for Efficient Multimodal Retrieval

definitions.text: A dataset with 250 prompts designed to evaluate the factuality of models in long-form generation tasks.
definitions.paper: Disambiguating Reference in Visually Grounded Dialogues through Joint Modeling of Textual and Multimodal Semantic Structures

relationships.paper: LIFBench: Evaluating the Instruction Following Performance and Stability of Large Language Models in Long-Context Scenarios
relationships.evidence: Factuality hallucination benchmark: BioGEN (Min et al., 2023) and LongFact (Wei et al., 2024b); (Section 4.1)
